channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NATO will step up Baltic Sea patrols as Finland probes the possible sabotage of undersea cables
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte says the military alliance will step up patrols in the Baltic Sea area as Finnish investigators work to establish whether a ship linked to Russia sabotaged undersea cables there this week.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about South Korean acting President Han's impeachment
item.description: The impeachment of South Korea’s acting President Han Duck-soo has plunged the country into further political turmoil, coming less than two weeks after lawmakers impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government placed sanctions on seven companies on Friday in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory. The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: U.S. and Azerbaijani officials have said weapons fire may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner that crashed on Wednesday, killing 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli soldiers burn a hospital in Gaza
item.description: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israeli soldiers stormed and burned a hospital after forcibly removing staff and patients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly voted Friday to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo despite vehement protests by governing party lawmakers, further deepening the country’s political crisis set off by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning imposition of m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran regime under 'immense pressure' amid incoming Trump admin policies, regional losses, economic woes
item.description: As a result of the Iranian regime’s financing of Islamist terrorist organization and its economic corruption, the people of the Mideast nation are facing widespread gas and power outages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Armed for survival: How Oct 7 Hamas massacre transformed gun culture in Israel
item.description: In the wake of the unprecedented October. 7 Hamas terrorist attacks, Israelis are having to grapple with the new reality of firearm ownership in a country where owning guns was once uncommon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen on Thursday targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital and multiple ports, while the World Health Organization's director-general said the bombardment occurred nearby as he prepared to board a flight in Sanaa, with a crew member injured.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is known about a plane crash in Kazakhstan as experts suspect Russian air defense fire
item.description: Aviation experts said Thursday an Azerbaijani airliner that crashed in Kazakhstan most likely went down after being hit by Russian air defense systems.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden arrives in St. Croix for last vacation as president
item.description: President Biden on Thursday arrived in St. Croix for his last vacation as president before leaving office next month. The president left the White House the day after Christmas with first lady Jill Biden. He arrived in St. Croix in the U.S. Virgin Islands around 2 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Grandson of former commandant of Auschwitz on rise of antisemitism, his life as a pastor
item.description: Kai Höss, the grandson of former Auschwitz Commandant Rudolf Höss, speaks with Fox News Digital about antisemitism today and how he reconciles his Christian faith with his grandfather's past.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia arrests 4 suspects accused of plotting to kill top military officers on Ukraine's orders
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's top security agency said Thursday that it has arrested several suspects accused of involvement in an alleged Ukrainian plot to assassinate senior military officers, an announcement that follows the killing of a top Russian general last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Incoming Trump admin, Congress showdown looms with South Africa over support for Russia, US foes
item.description: Senators and analysts tell Fox News Digital the incoming Trump administration could shut down South Africa’s AGOA trade deals if Pretoria doesn’t review its relations with U.S. foes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles uses Christmas address to thank medical workers
item.description: King Charles III used his annual Christmas address to thank the medical workers who have provided care for the royal family and hailed the humanitarian workers “tirelessly” providing aid to those engulfed in conflict around the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russia being blamed for Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed hundreds of miles off course, killing dozens
item.description: An Azerbaijan Airlines flight from the Azerbaijani capital of Baku to Russia crashed, hospitalizing survivors while dozens are feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope calls for end to global wars, divisions in Christmas message
item.description: Pope Francis called for an end to global wars and divisiveness in his annual Christmas Day message, given Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: Russia targets Ukrainian energy infrastructure on Christmas Day
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a massive missile and drone barrage targeting Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Wednesday, striking a thermal power plant and prompting Ukrainians to take shelter in metro stations on Christmas morning.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US military carries out airstrike in Syria, killing 2 ISIS operatives
item.description: The U.S. military conducted an airstrike on Monday in Syria, where they killed a pair of ISIS operatives, wounded another and destroyed a truckload of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police officer dressed as the 'Grinch' steals Christmas spirit during drug bust
item.description: The Peruvian National Police canceled Christmas early for three alleged drug traffickers during a holiday-themed takedown that was captured on camera.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO leaders predict era of 2% defense spending 'probably history' as Trump reportedly floats higher target
item.description: European leaders are making clear ahead of the Trump presidency that they plan to spend more on defense as Russia remains a top threat and as security concerns in the Middle East and North Africa escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christmas in Puerto Rico is a 45-day celebration with caroling, festive decorations, family feasts and more
item.description: Christmas in Puerto Rico is spent with loved ones singing carols, sitting around the table for a meal and exchanging gifts. There celebrations last from Thanksgiving to mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
